hook: Luca arrives at an old abandoned house for his first day of work and is given one instruction: throw everything away.
goal: Clear the rooms of the house as instructed by Ray Brennan.

start_state.location: Front door of the abandoned house (loc_001)
knowledge: Ray Brennan hired him for the weekend to clear the house
The house has been empty for a long time
He must bag everything and throw it away — Ray was clear about this
He does not know the name of the family who lived there

continuity_rules: This is Episode 1 — there are no prior events to reference or avoid
Do not show or hint at the contents of the metal box
Do not use the family name 'Cole' anywhere in the narrative — Luca has not learned it yet
key_events: Ray meets Luca at the front door, hands him the key, and says the same thing twice: throw everything away, do not keep anything. Luca asks who lived here. Ray says 'an old family' and walks back to his car without turning around. His hurry leaves Luca with more questions than answers.

Luca works alone in the back room, shifting old boxes and a broken chair. In the corner, under a pile of dusty curtains, his hand finds something hard and smooth — an old video camera. He opens the small door on its side: a tape is still inside.

Luca tries to turn the camera on. Nothing happens. The battery is dead. He holds the camera for a moment, then puts it in his work bag. He does not throw it away. He finishes the afternoon, locks the house, and takes the camera home.

At his flat, Luca searches a box of old cables until he finds one that fits the camera. He charges it for one hour. Then he presses play. The tape shows a man and a woman inside a room — the same dark wooden floor, the same pale walls. The man sets a metal box on the floor, lifts a loose board, pushes the box underneath, and stamps the board flat. The woman stands near the door, watching. She looks afraid. The tape ends after two minutes.
major_reveal: A tape in the old camera shows two people hiding a locked metal box under a loose floorboard in the very room where Luca was working.
new_thread_created: Is the metal box still under the floorboard in the back room of the abandoned house?
tone_guardrails: Ray's evasiveness must feel like ordinary human caution, not theatrical villainy
The camera discovery should feel like a routine find that quietly becomes important
Only one revelation: the tape showing the hidden box — do not add a second surprise
forbidden_moves: Do not use the family name 'Cole' — Luca does not know it yet; save it for the box discovery in Episode 2
Do not show or hint at what is inside the metal box
Do not let Ray explain anything about the house, the family, or why he wants everything thrown away
ending_hook: The floor in the tape is the same floor Luca stood on all day. Is the box still there?
